rules.name: Row count within expected range
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 2500 OR cnt > 3500 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.college_scorecard_institution)
rules.description: Total row count should be between 2,500 and 3,500. Current count is 3,039 after PREDDEG=3 OR ICLEVEL=1 filtering. The spec originally estimated 5,000-8,000 but EDA shows the actual filtered count is far lower. Range allows ~15% variation for annual data changes.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Record Count (filtered): 3,039.' Spec originally estimated ~6,500 but that was the unfiltered count (6,429). After PREDDEG=3 OR ICLEVEL=1 filtering, 3,039 rows remain. Range 2,500-3,500 provides headroom for annual institution openings/closures.

rules.name: unitid uniqueness
rules.sql: SELECT unitid, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM raw.college_scorecard_institution GROUP BY unitid HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per institution (UNITID). Any duplicate UNITID indicates an ingestor dedup failure. EDA confirmed 0 duplicates across 3,039 rows.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Distinct UNITIDs (filtered): 3,039. Duplicate UNITIDs: 0.' Domain context: 'UNITID is the sole grain key, guaranteed unique (3,039 rows, 3,039 distinct UNITIDs, 0 duplicates).' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: unitid not null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.college_scorecard_institution WHERE unitid IS NULL
rules.description: UNITID is the required grain field and join key to existing field-of-study data. Must be present in every row. EDA shows 0% null rate.
rules.evidence: EDA: UNITID is 100% populated across 3,039 rows. Domain context: 'UNITID must be a positive integer. IPEDS institutional identifiers are assigned as positive integers in the 6-digit range.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: control valid values
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.college_scorecard_institution WHERE control NOT IN (1, 2, 3)
rules.description: CONTROL must be 1 (Public), 2 (Private nonprofit), or 3 (Private for-profit). This field routes the Silver unified net price logic. Any other value would break downstream transformations.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'CONTROL Distribution: 1=867 (28.5%), 2=1,754 (57.7%), 3=418 (13.8%). 100% populated.' Domain context: 'Only three institution control types exist.' Zero violations in 3,039 rows. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: costt4_a range check
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.college_scorecard_institution WHERE costt4_a IS NOT NULL AND (costt4_a < 5000 OR costt4_a > 100000)
rules.description: When present, average cost of attendance (academic year) must be between $5,000 and $100,000. The observed range is $6,362-$87,804. The $5K-$100K bounds provide headroom for future drift while catching data corruption.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'COSTT4_A: Min=$6,362, Max=$87,804, Median=$30,288.' The $5,000 lower bound accommodates schools slightly below the current minimum. The $100,000 upper bound provides 14% headroom above the max. Threshold: 0 violations in 2,192 non-null rows.

rules.name: npt4_pub range check
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.college_scorecard_institution WHERE npt4_pub IS NOT NULL AND (npt4_pub < -5000 OR npt4_pub > 60000)
rules.description: When present, average net price for public institutions must be between -$5,000 and $60,000. Negative values are legitimate -- they occur when grant/scholarship aid exceeds total cost of attendance. The spec originally used $0 lower bound but EDA found 3 public schools with negative net prices.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'NPT4_PUB: Min=-$1,180 (Skyline College), Max=$32,598.' Three public schools have negative net prices (San Diego Mesa -$904, Skyline College -$1,180, St Petersburg College -$52). Domain context: 'Aid exceeds COA for average student. Legitimate.' Lower bound -$5,000 provides margin. Upper bound $60,000 provides generous headroom above max of $32,598. Threshold: 0 violations in 774 non-null rows.

rules.name: npt4_priv range check
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.college_scorecard_institution WHERE npt4_priv IS NOT NULL AND (npt4_priv < -5000 OR npt4_priv > 80000)
rules.description: When present, average net price for private institutions must be between -$5,000 and $80,000. Negative values are allowed to match the public rule pattern, though the current private minimum is $1,525 (positive). Quintile-level private values do go negative (MIT Q1 at -$4,129).
rules.evidence: EDA: 'NPT4_PRIV: Min=$1,525, Max=$77,180.' While the average net price is currently positive for all private institutions, the quintile fields show negatives (e.g., MIT Q1 at -$4,129). Using -$5,000 lower bound for consistency with public rule and future-proofing. Upper bound $80,000 provides margin above max $77,180. Threshold: 0 violations in 1,459 non-null rows.

rules.name: tuitionfee_in range check
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.college_scorecard_institution WHERE tuitionfee_in IS NOT NULL AND (tuitionfee_in < 0 OR tuitionfee_in > 75000)
rules.description: When present, in-state tuition and fees must be between $0 and $75,000. The observed range is $600-$69,330. P1 because tuition is not used in the core ROI formula. Upper bound raised from spec's $65,000 because EDA max ($69,330) exceeds it.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'TUITIONFEE_IN: Min=$600, Max=$69,330, Median=$15,222.' The spec's $65,000 upper bound would reject the observed max. Upper bound set to $75,000 to accommodate current data plus drift. Threshold: 0 violations in 2,518 non-null rows.

rules.name: roomboard_on range check
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.college_scorecard_institution WHERE roomboard_on IS NOT NULL AND (roomboard_on < 1000 OR roomboard_on > 30000)
rules.description: When present, on-campus room and board must be between $1,000 and $30,000. The spec originally used $3,000 floor but EDA found a legitimate minimum of $1,000 (partial-year or subsidized housing). Upper bound raised from spec's $25,000 because EDA max is $29,874.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'ROOMBOARD_ON: Min=$1,000, Max=$29,874, Median=$12,040.' Domain context: 'Spec DQ floor of $3,000 will reject this legitimate value.' Lower bound set to $1,000 to match observed minimum. Upper bound set to $30,000 to accommodate observed max of $29,874 plus margin. Threshold: 0 violations in 1,719 non-null rows.

rules.name: COA coverage (costt4_a or costt4_p non-null)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (CAST(coa_present AS DOUBLE) / CAST(total AS DOUBLE)) < 0.70 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS total, SUM(CASE WHEN costt4_a IS NOT NULL OR costt4_p IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS coa_present FROM raw.college_scorecard_institution)
rules.description: At least 70% of rows must have at least one cost of attendance field (costt4_a or costt4_p) non-null. The spec originally assumed 90% but EDA shows only 73.5% have COA data. Missing COA is concentrated in PREDDEG=0 (not classified) and PREDDEG=4 (graduate-dominant) institutions captured by the ICLEVEL=1 filter.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'COSTT4_A non-null: 2,192 (72.1%), COSTT4_P non-null: 41 (1.3%). Combined: 2,233 of 3,039 = 73.5%.' Domain context: '806 institutions (26.5%) have neither COSTT4_A nor COSTT4_P. Concentrated in PREDDEG=0 (288) and PREDDEG=4 (280).' Threshold: 70% (conservative floor below observed 73.5%).

rules.name: Public institution net price coverage
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (CAST(has_npt4_pub AS DOUBLE) / CAST(total_public AS DOUBLE)) < 0.75 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN control = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS total_public, SUM(CASE WHEN control = 1 AND npt4_pub IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS has_npt4_pub FROM raw.college_scorecard_institution)
rules.description: At least 75% of public institutions (CONTROL=1) should have npt4_pub populated. The spec originally assumed 80% but the actual rate is 89.3%, so 75% provides a conservative floor. P1 because coverage gaps are expected for some institutions.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'CONTROL=1 (Public): 867 total, 774 with NPT4_PUB (89.3%).' Spec assumed >=80% which would pass, but 75% provides better margin against future data refreshes where coverage may dip. Threshold: 75% of public institutions.

rules.name: Private institution net price coverage
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (CAST(has_npt4_priv AS DOUBLE) / CAST(total_private AS DOUBLE)) < 0.65 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN control = 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS total_private, SUM(CASE WHEN control = 2 AND npt4_priv IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS has_npt4_priv FROM raw.college_scorecard_institution)
rules.description: At least 65% of private nonprofit institutions (CONTROL=2) should have npt4_priv populated. The spec originally assumed 80% but EDA shows only 70.6% for CONTROL=2. The 65% threshold provides a safety margin. For-profit (CONTROL=3) is excluded from this rule due to known poor reporting (52.6%).
rules.evidence: EDA: 'CONTROL=2 (Private nonprofit): 1,754 total, 1,239 with NPT4_PRIV (70.6%).' Domain context: 'Private nonprofit coverage below spec. Lower to >= 65%.' For-profit excluded because EDA shows only 52.6% coverage -- a separate lower threshold would be needed. Threshold: 65% of CONTROL=2 institutions.

rules.name: Quintile span monotonicity (Q1 <= Q5)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM raw.college_scorecard_institution WHERE ((control = 1 AND npt41_pub IS NOT NULL AND npt45_pub IS NOT NULL AND npt41_pub > npt45_pub) OR (control IN (2, 3) AND npt41_priv IS NOT NULL AND npt45_priv IS NOT NULL AND npt41_priv > npt45_priv))
rules.description: Where both Q1 (lowest income, $0-$30K) and Q5 (highest income, $110K+) net prices are non-null, Q1 should be less than or equal to Q5. Lower-income families generally receive more aid. Adjacent-pair monotonicity (Q[n] <= Q[n+1]) is NOT enforced because EDA shows 37.9% Q1>Q2 inversions in private schools -- a known College Scorecard pattern where merit aid to Q2 can exceed need-based aid for Q1.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Full-span inversions (Q1 > Q5): Public 7 of 713 (1.0%), Private 34 of 1,073 (3.2%).' Total expected violations: ~41 of ~1,786 rows with both Q1 and Q5 populated. Threshold: 50 violations (provides margin above observed 41). Adjacent-pair monotonicity rejected per EDA: 'Q1 > Q2 inversions: 37.9% for private. Do NOT use adjacent-pair monotonicity rules.'
